source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: For an 8-hour modelling-only engineering-science benchmark, submit an analysis-driven preliminary design of a 20 m single-span pedestrian footbridge over a river crossing on a city council pedestrian network, carrying a 3 m wide deck. The bridge is a simply-supported steel Warren truss explicitly modelled as a multi-part assembly with top chords, bottom chords, diagonal web members, vertical web members, timber deck planks, transverse cross-beams connecting the two truss planes, and bolted / welded connection plates at panel nodes. Top and bottom chords are in AS/NZS 3679.1 Grade 300 steel (Young's modulus 200 GPa, Poisson 0.30, yield 300 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3). Timber deck planking is LVL (Young's modulus 13 GPa, design tension parallel to grain 22 MPa, density 600 kg/m^3). Design loads per AS/NZS 1170.1 pedestrian crowd: (LC1) uniformly distributed pedestrian live load 5 kPa on the full 20 m x 3 m deck; (LC2) concentrated load 10 kN on a 0.5 m x 0.5 m patch at mid-span deck; (LC3) pedestrian dynamic vertical: 280 N at first-mode frequency 2 Hz applied at mid-span to check serviceability; (LC4) wind lateral on elevation: 1.0 kPa on the full truss side elevation per AS/NZS 1170.2 region A7 ultimate. Pass/fail: (R1) max truss chord axial stress under LC1+LC2 below 200 MPa (yield 300 / 1.5), (R2) mid-span vertical deflection under LC1 below L/500 = 40 mm, (R3) vertical acceleration under LC3 below 0.5 m/s^2 per AS 5100.2 pedestrian comfort, (R4) lateral displacement under LC4 below L/400 = 50 mm. Verification is performed on the assembled multi-body STEP skeleton with representative nodal connection stiffness; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Because organizers mark the analysis narrative rather than a 3D geometry, the primary deliverable is an 8-page analytical report; the STEP AP242 skeleton model is emitted so the schema remains uniform with the rest of the benchmark set.
short_prompt:
For an 8-hour modelling-only engineering-science benchmark, submit the preliminary design of a 20 m single-span pedestrian footbridge carrying a 3 m wide deck, delivered as a multi-part simply-supported steel Warren truss.

Chords are AS/NZS 3679.1 Grade 300 steel (E 200 GPa, Poisson 0.30, yield 300 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3); timber deck planking is LVL (E 13 GPa, tension parallel to grain 22 MPa, density 600 kg/m^3).

Load cases per AS/NZS 1170.
- LC1 uniform pedestrian live load 5 kPa on the full 20 m x 3 m deck.
- LC2 concentrated load 10 kN on a 0.5 x 0.5 m patch at mid-span.
- LC3 pedestrian dynamic vertical: 280 N at first-mode frequency 2 Hz at mid-span.
- LC4 wind lateral on elevation: 1.0 kPa on the full truss side elevation per AS/NZS 1170.2 region A7 ultimate.

Mid-span deflection limit L/500 = 40 mm; lateral L/400 = 50 mm; vertical accel under LC3 <= 0.5 m/s^2 per AS 5100.2.

prompt.title: Engineering-science pedestrian bridge model (multi-part Warren truss)
prompt.brief: 20 m simply-supported steel Warren truss + LVL deck modelled as a multi-part assembly of chords, web diagonals / verticals, cross-beams, deck planks, and connection plates.

parts.name: top_chord_member
parts.role: upper chord forming the compression flange of each truss plane

parts.name: bottom_chord_member
parts.role: lower chord forming the tension flange of each truss plane

parts.name: web_diagonal_member
parts.role: diagonal web member forming the Warren pattern between chords

parts.name: web_vertical_member
parts.role: vertical web member at panel nodes (if used in the Warren variant)

parts.name: cross_beam
parts.role: transverse beam connecting the two truss planes and supporting deck planks

parts.name: deck_plank
parts.role: LVL timber deck plank running between cross-beams

parts.name: connection_plate
parts.role: bolted / welded gusset plate at each panel node tying chords and web members together

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 20 m span x 3 m deck width x team-selected truss depth
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X along span, Y transverse across deck, Z vertical

boundary_conditions.interfaces: chord-web connections via gusset plates with bolted / welded stiffness; cross-beams bolted to bottom chord nodes; deck planks pinned to cross-beams

prompt.objective: size truss and deck to pass AS/NZS 1170 serviceability and strength gates with minimum steel tonnage

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

interfaces.load_path: top chord / diagonal axial load into gusset
bottom chord / diagonal axial load into gusset
cross-beam reaction into bottom chord node

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Warren trusses are explicitly multi-member skeletons (chords + diagonals + verticals) connected by gusset plates at panel nodes, tied across the deck width by cross-beams and deck planks.
notes.speculation_caveats: Panel count, diagonal angle, and vertical presence are team-selectable; materials, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
